Title: Fluctuations in nitrate availability impact cytokinin biosynthesis through histone modifications of IPT3 in Arabidopsis roots for growth acclimation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root-to-shoot cytokinin transport supporting shoot growth under nitrate
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed during starvation and induced within 15 min of nitrate resupply; sustained induction after 3 h depends on IPT3-derived CKs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as major root-to-shoot CK transporter whose sustained induction under nitrate resupply depends on IPT3-derived CK accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in WT, ipt3-2, iptTM, IPT3-RFP under nitrate fluctuation and CK treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABCG14 is rapidly nitrate inducible and starvation repressible. 2. Early induction occurs largely independently of IPT3, but sustained induction after 3 h requires IPT3-derived CKs. 3. IPT3 overexpression elevates ABCG14 expression, linking CK biosynthesis to CK transport.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess transport could shift CK partitioning and growth balance; reduced expression may limit shoot recovery after nitrate resupply
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target together with IPT3/CYP735A2 to enhance shoot-directed nitrogen satiety signaling, but requires balance to preserve root growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats ABCG14 as a downstream transport component coordinated by IPT3-dependent CK accumulation, not as a primary focal gene.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic regulation shown in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native IPT3 promoter in IPT3-tagRFP complementation/overexpression line
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: iP-type cytokinin biosynthesis upstream of tZ-type branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an isopentenyl group to adenine nucleotides to produce isopentenyladenosine riboside phosphate precursors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cytokinin production and thereby enables downstream tZ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Balances root and shoot growth during nitrate fluctuation; restricts primary root growth and promotes shoot growth under nitrate supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced in roots by nitrate resupply within 15 min; repressed within 2 h of nitrate starvation; expressed in phloem companion cells and weakly near root tip/transition zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression responds dynamically to nitrate fluctuation; strongest focus in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrate sufficiency, 3-day nitrate starvation with KCl replacement, and nitrate resupply time course
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root cytokinin accumulation; maintains root/shoot biomass balance; represses primary root elongation and supports shoot growth in response to nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of nitrate-dependent cytokinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ipt3-2 and ipt3 ipt5 ipt7 mutant analysis; IPT3-tagRFP complementation/overexpression; RT-qPCR; cytokinin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS; growth phenotyping; ChIP-qPCR of H3K4me3/H3K27me3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT3 transcript dynamics closely parallel nitrate-dependent changes in root cytokinin content. 2. Loss of IPT3 causes a starvation-like growth pattern with elevated root/shoot ratio even under nitrate supply. 3. IPT3 induction after nitrate resupply is required for cytokinin recovery, repression of root growth, and stimulation of shoot growth. 4. IPT3 chromatin is dynamically regulated by H3K4me3 and H3K27me3 under fluctuating nitrate.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced IPT3 activity leads to constitutively high root/shoot ratio, longer primary roots, and reduced shoot growth; overexpression elevates CK and more strongly restricts root growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to tune cytokinin output and improve growth acclimation to fluctuating nitrogen; should be engineered with spatial/inducible control to avoid excessive root growth inhibition or shoot-root imbalance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IPT3-RFP behaved as an IPT3-overexpressing line while retaining nitrate-responsive repression and induction. Sustained induction of CYP735A2 and ABCG14 after nitrate resupply depends on IPT3-derived CKs.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: IPT3 transcription is controlled by antagonistic histone marks: H3K4me3 promotes expression, while H3K27me3 represses it. Nitrate starvation triggers early H3K4me3 loss and later H3K27me3 deposition; nitrate resupply triggers rapid H3K27me3 removal followed by H3K4me3 gain.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of iP-type cytokinin precursor iPRP into tZRP in the trans-zeatin branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of tZ-type cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports systemic shoot growth signaling and contributes to root-shoot balance under nitrate supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, with contribution to root-to-shoot transport
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed during nitrogen starvation and induced within 15 min of nitrate resupply; sustained induction after 3 h depends on IPT3-derived CKs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrate starvation/resupply; cytokinin treatment in absence of nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to tZ-type cytokinin accumulation; its sustained nitrate induction depends on IPT3-derived CK and is elevated in IPT3-RFP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under nitrate fluctuation in WT, ipt3-2, iptTM, IPT3-RFP and chromatin mutants; cytokinin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP735A2 is rapidly induced by nitrate and repressed by starvation. 2. Sustained induction after nitrate resupply is reduced in ipt3-related mutants, indicating dependence on IPT3-derived CK. 3. Elevated IPT3 expression enhances CYP735A2 expression and tZ accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered expression may shift iP-to-tZ conversion and cytokinin composition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with IPT3 stacking to enhance tZ-type cytokinin output and systemic nitrogen-growth signaling, but must be balanced to avoid excessive shoot-promoting CK effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats CYP735A2 mainly as a downstream CK-biosynthetic target coordinated by IPT3, not as the primary experimental focus.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Indirect evidence only; expression is affected downstream of IPT3 chromatin regulation and in some chromatin mutants.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls cytokinin biosynthesis output that shapes root/shoot growth acclimation to nitrate fluctuation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis via chromatin control of IPT3 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Removes activating H3K4me3 mark to repress IPT3 transcription during nitrate starvation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses IPT3 during early starvation by promoting H3K4me3 demethylation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed among KDM5 genes and significantly induced after 2 h starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full early repression of IPT3 during nitrate starvation; mutation elevates IPT3 expression and H3K4me3 at IPT3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent repressor contributing to rapid cytokinin downregulation during nitrate withdrawal
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: jmj14-1 mutant; RT-qPCR of IPT3; ChIP-qPCR of H3K4me3/H3K27me3 at IPT3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JMJ14 is induced by nitrate starvation with timing matching early IPT3 repression. 2. jmj14-1 retains higher IPT3 expression and H3K4me3 at IPT3 under starvation. 3. JMJ14 acts as one H3K4me3 demethylase for early IPT3 repression, though redundancy is likely.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing JMJ14 activity may maintain cytokinin biosynthesis during starvation and impair adaptive root foraging or stress acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to modulate starvation-induced cytokinin repression, but deployment may compromise normal stress-responsive growth adjustment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not sufficient alone to explain earliest IPT3 repression; additional repressors or redundant JMJs likely contribute.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct regulator of H3K4me3 demethylation at IPT3.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SET-domain Polycomb-group histone methyltransferase; PRC2 component
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to adaptive suppression of cytokinin biosynthesis during prolonged nitrate starvation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis via stable chromatin repression of IPT3
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Deposits repressive H3K27me3 at IPT3 during prolonged starvation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Maintains/stabilizes IPT3 repression after starvation-induced shutdown
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CLF transcript significantly induced after 3 days starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major H3K27me3 methyltransferase at IPT3; clf-29 strongly reduces H3K27me3 deposition at IPT3 and alters cytokinin composition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Stable repressor that locks IPT3 locus during prolonged nitrate deprivation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: clf-29 mutant; ProCLF:CFP:CLF complementation background reference; ChIP-qPCR for H3K27me3/H3K4me3 and CLF binding; RT-qPCR; cytokinin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CLF binds the IPT3 locus predominantly under nitrate-starved conditions. 2. clf-29 largely abolishes H3K27me3 deposition at IPT3. 3. CLF secures stable IPT3 repression during prolonged starvation, although H3K27me3 loss alone is insufficient to fully reactivate IPT3. 4. clf-29 alters iP-to-tZ cytokinin balance under nitrate supply.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weakening CLF-mediated repression could disrupt stress-associated chromatin locking and broader developmental programs due to PRC2 pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low direct translational specificity because PRC2 has broad developmental roles; targeted locus-specific epigenome editing would be preferable over global CLF manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Prolonged nitrate starvation and nitrate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of H3K27me3 at IPT3 does not by itself restore expression during starvation, indicating H3K27me3 mainly maintains rather than establishes repression.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct writer of H3K27me3 at IPT3.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enable cytokinin biosynthesis reactivation after nitrate resupply, supporting growth recovery
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis via chromatin reactivation of IPT3
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Remove repressive H3K27me3 from IPT3 upon nitrate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activate IPT3 by H3K27me3 demethylation during early nitrate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: REF6 induced within 15 min of nitrate resupply; JMJ30 also induced later, but mutant tested was ref6 elf6-3 jmj13
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for early IPT3 reinduction and normal CK recovery after nitrate resupply; triple mutant fails to reduce H3K27me3 and shows reduced iP- and tZ-type CKs upon resupply
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential early unlockers of IPT3 chromatin during nitrate repletion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ref6 elf6-3 jmj13 triple mutant; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR of H3K27me3/H3K4me3 at IPT3; cytokinin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF6/ELF6/JMJ13 are required for H3K27me3 removal from IPT3 after nitrate resupply. 2. The triple mutant shows weak or absent IPT3 induction and reduced CK accumulation upon nitrate resupply. 3. These demethylases mediate the early phase of IPT3 activation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Blocking these demethylases may impair recovery from nitrate starvation and broader developmental processes because of functional redundancy and pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic lever for faster nitrogen recovery responses, but locus-specific approaches are preferable to whole-gene manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper demonstrates redundancy using triple mutant; exact individual contribution of REF6 versus ELF6 versus JMJ13 remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct erasers of H3K27me3 at IPT3.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cytokinin (iP- and tZ-type, especially tZ-type recovery)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cytokinin production under nitrate supply and during recovery after nitrate resupply, promoting shoot growth and limiting root growth
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis via activation of IPT3 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates IPT3 and is required for the second, stronger phase of nitrate-induced IPT3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ATX1 was the only tested H3K4 methyltransferase induced within 3 h of nitrate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for H3K4me3 enrichment at IPT3, full IPT3 expression under nitrate, the second phase of IPT3 induction after resupply, and normal CK accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key activator driving the stronger late nitrate-response phase of cytokinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: atx1-2 mutant; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR of H3K4me3/H3K27me3 at IPT3; cytokinin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATX1 is the predominant H3K4 methyltransferase acting at IPT3. 2. atx1-2 reduces basal IPT3 expression and abolishes the second phase of IPT3 induction after nitrate resupply. 3. Loss of ATX1 lowers active CK accumulation, particularly tZ-type CKs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing ATX1 may impair nitrate-responsive shoot recovery and alter broader trithorax-regulated development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to enhance nitrogen-responsive cytokinin output, but targeted tuning of IPT3 chromatin would likely be safer than global ATX1 manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constant nitrate and nitrate resupply after starvation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initial weak IPT3 induction after nitrate resupply still occurs in atx1-2, showing ATX1 is specifically required for the later amplification phase.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct writer of H3K4me3 at IPT3.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to nitrate-triggered cytokinin biosynthesis that supports growth acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of IPT3 downstream of H3K27me3 removal and upstream of full H3K4me3 enrichment
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promote proper IPT3 induction after nitrate resupply; not required for H3K27me3 removal
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NLP7 is nitrate-responsive and known nitrate sensor/effector; in this paper functional analysis used nlp6 nlp7-1 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus in presence of nitrate (from cited literature); not directly assayed here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full nitrate-induced IPT3 expression, but chromatin unlocking by H3K27me3 demethylation still occurs without NLP6/NLP7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important but not sole determinant; contributes to proper IPT3 activation rather than chromatin unlocking
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nlp6 nlp7-1 double mutant; RT-qPCR and ChIP-qPCR for IPT3 H3K27me3/H3K4me3 after nitrate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP6/NLP7 are needed for full IPT3 induction after nitrate resupply. 2. H3K27me3 demethylation at IPT3 occurs normally in their absence. 3. NLP6/NLP7 therefore act downstream of H3K27me3 removal and upstream of full H3K4me3-mediated activation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may broadly alter nitrate signaling networks beyond cytokinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for improving nitrogen-use acclimation, but broad nitrate-network pleiotropy warrants caution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper indicates IPT3 is probably not a direct NLP7 target based on prior ChIP datasets; effect may be indirect and/or involve NLP6.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts in coordination with IPT3 chromatin state; not required for H3K27me3 removal but associated with reduced H3K4me3 enrichment in mutant.
